allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps critical-mass identify whether the threshold is real by looking at the ecosystem and the interactions that sustain momentum.
allies.source_quote: Critical Mass is a concept derived from systems thinking, providing a framework for seeing interrelationships rather than things.

allies.rationale_text: Feedback-loops explain how critical-mass turns from isolated activity into self-reinforcing growth once the threshold is crossed.
allies.source_quote: once the critical threshold is met, positive feedback loops (like network effects or momentum) accelerate growth

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias makes critical-mass overfit a threshold story onto weak evidence just because the team wants the pattern to be true.
antagonists.source_quote: critical-mass vs confirmation-bias: critical-mass conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when pattern-recognition bias causes teams to force a threshold framing onto problems that do not actually follow critical-mass dynamics.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Critical-mass conflicts with representativeness-bias when early adopter behavior is misread as durable mass adoption.
structured_tensions.source_quote: critical-mass vs representativeness-bias: critical-mass conflicts with representativeness-bias (representativeness bias) when early adopter behavior is misread as durable mass adoption.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Critical-mass conflicts with incentives when local actor payoffs discourage participation needed to reach threshold momentum.
structured_tensions.source_quote: critical-mass vs incentives: critical-mass conflicts with incentives (incentives) when local actor payoffs discourage participation needed to reach threshold momentum.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Critical-mass conflicts with confirmation-bias when pattern-recognition bias causes teams to force a threshold framing onto problems that do not actually follow critical-mass dynamics.
structured_tensions.source_quote: critical-mass vs confirmation-bias: critical-mass conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when pattern-recognition bias causes teams to force a threshold framing onto problems that do not actually follow critical-mass dynamics.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on threshold allies and explicit threshold-misread conflicts, so the file preserves those directly and avoids wider inferred relation sprawl.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor topology-like threshold overlays because this batch stops at first-order relation semantics.
open_questions: Momentum and Pareto are source-real allies, but systems-thinking and feedback-loops were the cleaner bounded anchors for this conservative pass.
